item.title: Mod Post - Open
item.content:encoded: Just noting that I've sourced the image to here and that it's under CC-BY-SA 3.0.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: nice job making sure the last 2 digits were 69

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Im glad this was good and not just porn

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't know why I never noticed this before, but please try not to bring up rape on articles on-site in a joking manner :o

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm pretty into weird stuff. This SCP is pretty much the one that brought me hear for [REASONS] so honestly, I felt that rewriting it was an honor.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Oh hey, it's good old 562, now with less overt fetish fuel!

Honestly, I kinda miss the gonzo days of SCP. It was fun reading the site back then. I understood the purge of edgelord anime SCPs, but then every other entry that didn't fit a fairly narrow definition of horror got purged too, and for a few years it was really boring here. And even when you started to get some variety it quickly became basically "Stargate meets Lovecraft", with all the interconnected alternate realities, eldritch societies and the like. Pitch Haven, Daevites, Sarkic stuff… it was just such a huge shift in tone. So I'm glad the gonzo stuff seems to be making a reappearance.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm trying to picture a mermaid army obliterating New York by chomping through the foundations….

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Honestly I was only mildly surprised to see the name of this article as I was scrolling through the list so it doesn't surprise me much. Also +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Originally a downvote, but there's enough creative effort put into the details to warrant a neutral vote.

The imagery is good, but at the end of the day these are just another variation on the very old hybridizer alien/monster trope. I guess it not being overly violent nor fatal to the host organism is a variation, but that's still not quite enough to truly interest me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
… That's a thing?

We have limestone-eating mermaids?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Downvote.

I just can't separate this article from the limestone-eating mermaids we already have, but crossed with facehuggers.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This makes me think of Octavia Butler's "Bloodchild" as revised by Clive Barker.
In a good way, I hasten to add. It started off sounding like a squicky D&D monster, and took a bullet train to Nightmare Fuel.
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Viscerally disturbing. Especially since I imagined it, for whatever reason, as being lobster-like, and had to deal with the phrase 'rape lobster' running through my brain to the tunes of the B-52s.
Yecchhh. +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
average instance of SCP-2869 measures 3.5 meters from head to tail, and stands 2 centimeters tall.

I'm relatively certain that the "centi" in the bolded bit shouldn't be there. That would be one flat caterpillar-centaur-thing.

Also, I'm not sure that "hexapedal" (six-footed) would be the correct term, since the things have four actual legs and four arms, for a total of eight limbs. Octopedal wouldn't work, since only four limbs are used for walking (as currently described, anyway). Humans are referred to as bipedal, despite having four limbs, since we only walk on two. Maybe just "eight-limbed"?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That's probably a good idea, yeah.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: So, do males give birth through their penis? You know what, I think it's better if you don't answer.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like this one, though I have a minor suggestion. The last paragraph where the size is given would make more sense as part of the first paragraph IMO, as given the comparison to a caterpillar in the first paragraph I had it pictured as 12-15cm tops until I got to the end. I would also make it clear that the host's sex doesn't matter for impregnation as, while it's sort of implied in the description, it isn't completely clear until the tests.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Huh… That's the site I'd been looking for a while ago x.x thanks, Deci!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: People can read the article here!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This article is a rewrite of the very first article I'd ever read on this site, which was deleted very early 2012, and was written by Le Blue Dude, under the number and name SCP-562 "Sentient Worms". It's a pretty significant article to my history! ^_^

EDIT: Also, thanks to Cyan, WJS, Shaggy, Avacyn, and a few others I can't remember off the top of my head(Sorry if you're one of 'em ;-; ) for giving me help on this draft!
